nativeRange.habitat: Coastal seafloors at depths of about 1–15 m, generally over sand and rubble adjacent to reefs.

decorPreferences: open swimming space along the bottom

diet.notes: Generally accepts a varied diet of meaty marine foods; mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, chopped seafood, and quality pellets/flakes are commonly recommended. Tends to do best with several small feedings per day.

avoidWith: large aggressive fish that may bully bottom-dwellers

summary: Indo-Pacific shrimp goby (Cryptocentrus pavoninoides), generally peaceful toward tankmates but territorial with its own kind; sand-dwelling burrower that often pairs with a pistol shrimp.
careNotes: Generally considered a hardy, beginner-friendly shrimp goby and one of the larger Cryptocentrus species, reaching around 12–15 cm. Sources broadly agree it is peaceful toward unrelated tankmates but strongly territorial toward conspecifics and other shrimp gobies, so it is normally kept singly or as an established mated pair. Has been reported to form the classic symbiotic relationship with pistol shrimp (Alpheus spp.) — the shrimp digs and maintains a burrow that the goby uses as a refuge and lookout post. A deep sandy substrate (a few centimetres at minimum) plus loose coral rubble is generally advised so the pair can construct stable burrows; ample open bottom is also recommended for foraging and territorial spacing. Like most shrimp gobies, this species is a known jumper and a tightly-sealed lid is strongly recommended. Cross-source notes: BlueZoo Aquatics rates temperament as 'peaceful' while Live Rock 'N Reef lists it as 'semi-aggressive' and Tank Facts emphasises territoriality toward its own kind — the consensus interpretation is peaceful community-wide but high conspecific aggression. Minimum tank size ranged 29–30 US gal (110–114 L) across the retail sources; given the species' adult size, active bottom use, and need for a sand/rubble territory, 120 L (and a longer footprint of roughly 90 cm) is recommended as a more realistic minimum. Has been reported to be aggressive toward very small ornamental shrimp, so pairing with delicate sexy shrimp or anemone shrimp is generally discouraged.

primary.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
primary.notes: Primary source after LiveAquaria did not list this species; BlueZoo is the listed manifest secondary and was used as primary for sizing/care level/temperament.

additional.name: Live Rock N Reef
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters (temperature 74–82°F, pH 7.9–8.5, SG 1.020–1.027), tank size (29 gal), reef-safe status, jumping behaviour, and sandy-substrate burrowing requirement.

additional.name: Tank Facts
additional.notes: Independent third source for water parameters (76–80°F, pH 7.8–8.4, SG 1.023–1.026), conspecific territoriality, jumping risk, and additional trade-name synonyms (Peacock Shrimpgoby, Yellow Barred Shrimp Goby).

additional.name: Wikipedia (Cryptocentrus pavoninoides)
additional.notes: Taxonomy (Gobiidae, Gobiiformes), maximum length 15 cm SL, depth range 1–15 m, native range Western Central Pacific, synonym Gobius pavoninoides Bleeker 1849.
